name: Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tornos offers diverse terrain, primarily characterized by its proximity to the Laguna de Gallocanta, a significant natural reserve. The region features a mix of flat, open landscapes around the lake and more undulating paths through agricultural areas and river valleys. Elevation gains are generally moderate, making it accessible for various skill levels. The trails often provide expansive views of the natural surroundings and local villages. Best mountain bike trails around Tornos The most popular mountain bike trail is Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular, an 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, circling the scenic natural reserve. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Pelardas por el Valle del Jiloca — circular desde Calamocha (BTT04), a moderate 32.0 miles (51.5 km) path. This route offers a longer ride through the Jiloca Valley with notable elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01), a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail leading through the natural reserve, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Tornos is defined by routes around the Laguna de Gallocanta, through the Jiloca Valley, and across open agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging, longer routes. The routes in Tornos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 6 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tornos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Tornos?
A: Tornos offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging, longer rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Tornos?
A: The terrain around Tornos is diverse, primarily featuring flat, open landscapes around the Laguna de Gallocanta, mixed with undulating paths through agricultural areas and river valleys. While elevation gains are generally moderate, some routes, particularly those extending into the Jiloca Valley or Sierra de Pelardas, offer more significant climbs and descents.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tornos?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Tornos is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially some snow, especially at higher elevations, though the region's open landscapes are generally accessible.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Tornos?
A: Yes, Tornos has trails suitable for beginners and families. The region offers easy loops with moderate elevation gains, particularly around the Laguna de Gallocanta, making them accessible for various skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Tornos?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Tornos are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999042462/reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-desde-tornos-btt01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)&lt;/a&gt;, both circling the scenic natural reserve.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking around Tornos?
A: While mountain biking around Tornos, you can encounter several natural attractions. The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a significant natural reserve. You might also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguilay Waterfall and Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing stop.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region around Tornos offers historical and cultural points of interest. You could pass through the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha&lt;/a&gt;, see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;, or spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement&lt;/a&gt; near the Gallocanta Lagoon.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints in Tornos?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Tornos offer expansive views of the natural surroundings, especially around the Laguna de Gallocanta. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory (Gallocanta Lagoon)&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight designed for observation, providing excellent vantage points.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Tornos typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations in Tornos vary significantly depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999042462/reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-desde-tornos-btt01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)&lt;/a&gt;, might require closer to 5 hours.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Tornos?
A: Yes, Tornos offers challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha – Luco de Jiloca loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 75 km route with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding ride through the Jiloca Valley.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Tornos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Tornos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 6 reviews. Over 300 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the natural reserve and the diverse options for different ability levels.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Tornos?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trail, Tornos and nearby villages generally offer parking options. For routes around the Laguna de Gallocanta, you can often find parking in the small settlements surrounding the lake, such as Tornos itself.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Tornos dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor trails in the Tornos region are generally dog-friendly, especially those in open natural areas like around the Laguna de Gallocanta. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs under control, especially near wildlife or agricultural land, and to check specific trail regulations if available.
tips:
The Gallocanta lagoon is the largest saltwater lagoon in Europe. A spectacular natural environment to enjoy nature and the popular cranes (there are viewpoints from where we can observe them with binoculars)
The colors that it gives us at sunset are beautiful.

Beautiful hermitage in a spectacular setting, such as the Gallocanta lagoon. One of the best places to watch birds. There are informative panels about the lagoon and the birds, a place to make a barbecue and an area to take refuge in case of a storm.

From this observatory we will have privileged views of the Gallocanta Lagoon from the heights, it is recommended to go with binoculars to see the birds up close. The trails that surround the lagoon are ideal for gravel or mountain biking.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement (Gallocanta Lagoon)
